title: Hamiltonian Simulation: The Heart of Quantum Computing
description: Master the fundamental problem of simulating quantum systems on quantum computers. Understand why Hamiltonian simulation was Feynman's original motivation for quantum computing and learn the theoretical foundations that underpin all quantum simulation algorithms.
learningObjectives: Explain why classical computers cannot efficiently simulate quantum systems
Derive the time evolution operator from the Schrodinger equation
Decompose arbitrary Hamiltonians into Pauli string representations
Analyze the computational complexity of exact vs. approximate simulation
Implement basic Hamiltonian simulation for small quantum systems
Understand the relationship between simulation error and circuit depth
